data.title: Add reference libraries accessible to agents during development workflows
data.description:
Create a structured collection of reference libraries that agents can consult during development workflows (implementation-plan, sprint, fix-bug, architect-foundation, etc.). These libraries provide domain-specific guidance that agents currently lack or must discover ad hoc via web search.

## Example Reference Libraries
- **Accessibility (a11y):** WCAG compliance requirements, ARIA patterns, semantic HTML guidance
- **CI Best Practices:** Pipeline design, caching strategies, test parallelization, artifact management
- **Architectural Patterns:** Existing patterns already in use in this project (extends the current architect-foundation detection)
- **Security:** OWASP top 10 mitigations, input validation patterns, auth/authz best practices
- **Performance:** Profiling guidance, caching strategies, database query optimization

## Requirements
- Reference libraries should be discoverable by agents at the point of need (e.g., implementation-plan consults the a11y library when generating tasks for UI work)
- Libraries should be project-configurable — teams can add, remove, or customize which references are active
- Content should be concise and actionable, not exhaustive — optimized for agent consumption within token budgets
